title: New EU Rules Stop Companies from Destroying Unsold Clothes
subtitle: The European Union is taking a major step to reduce waste by banning the destruction of new, unsold fashion items.

summary.en: The European Union has introduced a new ban on destroying unsold clothing and footwear. Large companies must now find ways to sell or donate items instead of throwing them away. This rule aims to reduce the massive amount of textile waste caused by fast fashion. Experts hope this will encourage a more sustainable way of producing and consuming goods. While some business groups support the goal, they warn that the new requirements will be difficult to manage.
summary.zh: 歐盟已針對未售出的衣物與鞋類實施新的銷毀禁令。大型企業現在必須設法銷售或捐贈商品，不得直接丟棄。此規定旨在減少快時尚造成的龐大紡織廢棄物。專家期望這能鼓勵更永續的生產與消費方式。儘管部分商業團體支持此目標，但也警告新規定在執行上將面臨困難。

paragraphs.en: Starting this July, a new rule across the European Union has changed how fashion companies handle unsold products. The EU has officially banned the destruction of new, unsold clothes and shoes. This move is part of a larger plan to cut down on waste and help the environment. Every year, hundreds of thousands of tons of clothing are destroyed in the EU because they are old, damaged, or simply returned by online shoppers who decided they did not want them.
paragraphs.zh: 從今年七月開始，歐盟實施了一項新規定，改變了時尚企業處理未售出產品的方式。歐盟已正式禁止銷毀全新但未售出的衣物與鞋類。此舉是減少浪費並保護環境的大型計畫之一。每年在歐盟境內，有數十萬噸的衣物因為過時、受損，或是單純被網購消費者退貨而遭到銷毀。

paragraphs.en: According to Deutsche Welle (DW), this regulation is part of the EU’s 'Ecodesign for Sustainable Products' law. The rules apply to large companies that have more than 250 employees and earn more than 50 million euros every year. Under this new system, these businesses are no longer allowed to burn or throw away products that they cannot sell. Instead, they must find other ways to deal with their stock, such as selling items at a discount, sending them to different markets, or giving them to charity organizations.
paragraphs.zh: 根據德國之聲（Deutsche Welle, DW）報導，這項規範是歐盟「永續產品生態設計法規」的一部分。該規定適用於員工超過兩百五十人且年營收超過五千萬歐元的企業。在新制度下，這些企業不得再焚燒或丟棄無法售出的產品。相反地，他們必須尋找其他處理庫存的方式，例如打折出售、轉銷至其他市場，或是捐贈給慈善機構。

paragraphs.en: There are only a few specific situations where companies are still allowed to destroy goods. As noted on the European Commission's website, items can only be destroyed if they are unsafe, damaged, fake, or if charities refuse to accept them. Additionally, companies must now keep careful records of what they throw away and publish an annual report about their waste. This law currently focuses on large businesses, but the EU plans to include medium-sized companies in these rules by 2030.
paragraphs.zh: 僅有少數特定情況下，企業仍被允許銷毀商品。根據歐盟執委會（European Commission）網站指出，唯有在商品不安全、受損、仿冒，或是慈善機構拒絕接收的情況下，才允許銷毀。此外，企業現在必須詳細記錄丟棄的物品，並發布年度廢棄物報告。這項法律目前針對大型企業，但歐盟計畫在二〇三〇年前將中型企業也納入規範。

paragraphs.en: The problem of 'fast fashion'—which refers to low-cost, mass-produced clothing—has grown quickly over the last ten years. The European Environment Agency (EEA) reports that between 4% and 9% of all unsold textile products are destroyed every year. This issue has become worse because of the rise in online shopping. When people buy clothes online, they often return them, and many of these returned items are not sold again. In fact, the EEA noted that one in five fashion items bought online is sent back to the store and eventually becomes waste.
paragraphs.zh: 「快時尚」——指低成本、大量生產的服飾——的問題在過去十年內迅速惡化。歐洲環境署（European Environment Agency, EEA）指出，每年有百分之四到百分之九的未售出紡織品遭到銷毀。隨著網路購物興起，此問題更趨嚴重。當人們在網路上購買衣物時，經常會選擇退貨，而許多退貨商品無法再次售出。事實上，歐洲環境署提到，每五件網購時尚商品中，就有一件會被退回商店，最終成為廢棄物。

paragraphs.en: This waste is a major concern because it harms the environment. When millions of clothes are burned, it releases greenhouse gases—gases that trap heat in the atmosphere and contribute to climate change. The EEA explained in a recent post that the EU wants to move toward a 'circular economy.' This is a system where products are designed to last a long time, and where people reuse, repair, and recycle materials instead of just throwing them away. The agency stated that this vision is now becoming a reality through these new laws.
paragraphs.zh: 這些廢棄物因危害環境而備受關注。當數百萬件衣物被焚燒時，會釋放溫室氣體，這些氣體會將熱量困在大氣層中，進而導致氣候變遷。歐洲環境署在近期的一篇文章中解釋，歐盟希望邁向「循環經濟」。這是一種將產品設計為長久耐用，並鼓勵人們重複使用、修復與回收材料，而非直接丟棄的系統。該機構表示，透過這些新法規，此願景正逐漸成為現實。

paragraphs.en: However, the fashion industry has different views on these changes. For a long time, companies have argued that it is often cheaper to destroy unsold goods than to store, repair, or discount them. Some businesses feel that the cost of logistics—the process of moving and managing goods—is too high to make reselling or donating every single item practical.
paragraphs.zh: 然而，時尚產業對這些變革持有不同看法。長期以來，企業一直主張銷毀未售出商品的成本，往往低於倉儲、維修或打折出售的成本。部分企業認為，物流——即搬運與管理商品的過程——成本過高，使得將每一件商品重新銷售或捐贈變得不切實際。

paragraphs.en: Stefan Genth, the Managing Director of the German Retail Federation (HDE), shared his thoughts on the new rules. According to DW, he said that consumers will likely benefit from the ban because more discounted clothes will appear in stores and second-hand markets. However, he also warned that the change will be a challenge for many shops. Genth explained that not all unsold goods can be easily donated or sold again. He pointed to problems like damaged packaging, a lack of demand for certain styles, or the fact that some items simply have very low value.
paragraphs.zh: 德國零售商協會（German Retail Federation, HDE）執行董事史蒂芬·根特（Stefan Genth）分享了他對新規則的看法。根據德國之聲報導，他表示消費者可能會從這項禁令中受益，因為商店與二手市場將出現更多折扣衣物。然而，他也警告這項改變對許多商店來說將是一項挑戰。根特解釋，並非所有未售出的商品都能輕易捐贈或再次銷售。他指出了包裝受損、特定款式需求不足，或是某些商品價值極低等問題。

paragraphs.en: As the EU moves forward with these laws, the focus remains on making the textile industry more sustainable. The regulation is a clear sign that the bloc is serious about reducing the impact of fashion on the planet. While companies will have to change how they operate, the goal is to ensure that the clothes we produce are used, not wasted. Whether this will lead to a major shift in how we shop remains to be seen, but it is a significant step toward a greener future for the fashion industry.
paragraphs.zh: 隨著歐盟持續推動這些法規，重點仍放在提升紡織產業的永續性。這項規範明確顯示歐盟致力於減少時尚產業對地球造成的影響。雖然企業必須改變營運方式，但目標是確保生產出的衣物能被使用，而非浪費。這是否會導致我們購物方式的重大轉變仍有待觀察，但這確實是時尚產業邁向綠色未來的重要一步。

keyTerms.definitionEn: To reduce the amount or size of something.
keyTerms.noteZh: 這是常見的片語，但在這裡指減少廢棄物。文中：This move is part of a larger plan to cut down on waste and help the environment.

keyTerms.definitionEn: The total amount of goods available for sale in a shop or business.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作動詞（儲存），這裡作名詞指店家的存貨。文中：Instead, they must find other ways to deal with their stock, such as selling items at a discount, sending them to different markets, or giving them to charity organizations.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To take action in order to solve a problem or handle a situation.
keyTerms.noteZh: 雖然字面簡單，但這裡指企業如何處置庫存。文中：Instead, they must find other ways to deal with their stock, such as selling items at a discount, sending them to different markets, or giving them to charity organizations.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To be not yet known or decided.
keyTerms.noteZh: 這是一個慣用語，用來表示結果尚未揭曉。文中：Whether this will lead to a major shift in how we shop remains to be seen, but it is a significant step toward a greener future for the fashion industry.

sources.name: Deutsche Welle (DW) Top
sources.title: EU ban on destroying unsold clothes takes effect

quiz.question: According to the article, under what condition are companies currently permitted to destroy unsold goods?

options.A: If the products are from a previous fashion season.
options.B: If the items are counterfeit, unsafe, or rejected by charities.
options.C: If the cost of storing or transporting the items is too high.
options.D: If the company has fewer than 250 employees.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。根據文章第三段，歐盟委員會明確指出，只有在商品不安全、損壞、仿冒（fake）或慈善機構拒絕接收的情況下，公司才被允許銷毀庫存。選項 A 是錯誤的，因為過季並非銷毀的合法理由；選項 C 雖然是企業抱怨的困難點，但並非法律允許的例外情況；選項 D 則混淆了適用法律的公司規模門檻與銷毀規定。

quiz.question: What can be inferred about the impact of online shopping on the fashion waste problem?

options.A: Online shopping has reduced the amount of textile waste by making logistics more efficient.
options.B: Online shopping is the primary reason why fashion companies have shifted to sustainable materials.
options.C: The convenience of online shopping contributes significantly to waste because of high return rates.
options.D: Online shopping has made it easier for companies to donate unsold goods to charity.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。根據第四段，文章指出網購的興起加劇了浪費問題，因為消費者常退貨，且許多退貨商品無法再次銷售，甚至五分之一的網購服飾最終成為廢棄物。選項 A 與文章意旨相反；選項 B 文中未提及網購促進了環保材質的使用；選項 D 則與退貨導致的過剩庫存問題無直接關聯。

quiz.question: In the final paragraph, what does the word 'bloc' refer to?

options.A: A large group of fashion retail companies.
options.B: The European Union as a political and economic entity.
options.C: A collection of waste management and recycling centers.
options.D: A specific type of sustainable textile material.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。在此語境下，「bloc」指的是歐盟（European Union），這是一個由多個國家組成的政治經濟共同體。選項 A 指的是時尚零售商，不符合句意；選項 C 與 D 均為錯誤的詞義解釋。在國際新聞中，常以「bloc」代指歐盟或類似的區域性組織。

quiz.question: What is the primary message of this article?

options.A: The EU is forcing all fashion companies to stop producing low-cost clothing immediately.
options.B: Large fashion companies are successfully lobbying to remove environmental regulations.
options.C: The EU is implementing new regulations to curb fashion waste and promote a circular economy.
options.D: Online shopping is the only sector of the fashion industry that needs to be regulated.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。全文的核心在於介紹歐盟針對時尚業 unsold 產品的新禁令，旨在減少浪費並推動「循環經濟」（circular economy）。選項 A 誇大了禁令範圍，並非禁止生產；選項 B 與文中提到的企業擔憂不符；選項 D 錯誤地將問題焦點僅限於網購，忽略了整體紡織業的規範。
